      <!DOCTYPE html>
      <html lang="en">
      <head>
      <meta charset="UTF-8">
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
      <title>DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28 Curses Proof Dossier v5</title>
      <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@400;600;700&family=Crimson+Pro:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,600;1,400&family=Cinzel:wght@400;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
      <style>
      :root {
      --gold: #C9A84C;
      --gold-dim: #8B6F00;
      --gold-bright: #E8C560;
      --bg-page: #0D0C0A;
      --bg-section: #131210;
      --bg-card: #1A1816;
      --bg-scripture: #1E1B14;
      --bg-divider: #111009;
      --text-primary: #EDE8DC;
      --text-secondary: #A89F88;
      --text-muted: #6B6456;
      --border: rgba(201,168,76,0.2);
      --border-bright: rgba(201,168,76,0.5);
      --tag-bg: rgba(201,168,76,0.08);
      --red-accent: #8B2020;
      --teal-accent: #1B5E5E;
      }
      * { margin:0; padding:0; box-sizing:border-box; }
      html { scroll-padding-top: 64px; }
      body { font-family:'Crimson Pro',Georgia,serif; background:var(--bg-page); color:var(--text-primary); font-size:19px; line-height:1.8; }
      .site-header { position:sticky; top:0; z-index:100; background:var(--bg-divider); border-bottom:1px solid var(--border-bright); padding:0 2rem; }
      .site-header-inner { max-width:1280px; margin:0 auto; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; height:54px; gap:1rem; }
      .site-title { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:12px; font-weight:600; color:var(--gold); letter-spacing:0.12em; white-space:nowrap; flex-shrink:0; }
      .progress-bar-wrap { flex:1; height:2px; background:rgba(255,255,255,0.05); border-radius:2px; overflow:hidden; max-width:200px; }
      .progress-bar-fill { height:100%; background:var(--gold); width:0%; transition:width 0.3s ease; }
      .section-indicator { font-size:12px; color:var(--text-muted); white-space:nowrap; min-width:80px; text-align:right; }
      .layout { max-width:1280px; margin:0 auto; display:grid; grid-template-columns:260px 1fr; min-height:calc(100vh - 54px); }
      .sidebar { position:sticky; top:54px; height:calc(100vh - 54px); overflow-y:auto; background:var(--bg-section); border-right:1px solid var(--border); padding:1rem 0; scrollbar-width:thin; scrollbar-color:var(--gold-dim) transparent; }
      .sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar { width:3px; }
      .sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background:var(--gold-dim); }
      .nav-label { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:9px; letter-spacing:0.2em; color:var(--text-muted); padding:0 1.25rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem; text-transform:uppercase; }
      .nav-item { display:block; padding:0.45rem 1.25rem; font-size:16px; color:var(--text-secondary); text-decoration:none; border-left:2px solid transparent; transition:all 0.2s; cursor:pointer; line-height:1.4; }
      .nav-item:hover { color:var(--text-primary); background:rgba(201,168,76,0.05); }
      .nav-item.active { color:var(--gold); border-left-color:var(--gold); background:rgba(201,168,76,0.06); }
      .nav-item .nav-num { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:11px; color:var(--gold-dim); display:block; margin-bottom:1px; }
      .nav-divider { height:1px; background:var(--border); margin:0.75rem 1.25rem; }
      .nav-sub { padding:0.3rem 1.25rem 0.3rem 1.5rem; font-size:16.5px; color:var(--text-muted); cursor:pointer; display:block; border-left:2px solid transparent; transition:all 0.2s; line-height:1.4; }
      .nav-sub:hover { color:var(--text-secondary); background:rgba(201,168,76,0.03); }
      .nav-sub.active { color:var(--gold-dim); border-left-color:var(--gold-dim); }
      .entry-nav { font-size:15px !important; padding:0.25rem 1.25rem 0.25rem 1.5rem !important; }
      .main { padding:2.5rem 3rem 6rem; overflow-x:hidden; }
      .title-block { text-align:center; padding:3rem 0 2.5rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); margin-bottom:2.5rem; }
      .dossier-eyebrow { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:10px; letter-spacing:0.3em; color:var(--gold); margin-bottom:1.5rem; display:block; }
      .dossier-title { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:42px; font-weight:700; color:var(--gold); line-height:1.2; margin-bottom:0.75rem; }
      .dossier-subtitle { font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-size:22px; color:var(--text-secondary); margin-bottom:1.5rem; }
      .dossier-meta { display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:1.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap; }
      .meta-pill { font-size:10px; font-family:'Cinzel',serif; letter-spacing:0.1em; color:var(--text-muted); border:1px solid var(--border); padding:0.25rem 0.7rem; border-radius:2px; }
      .doc-section { margin-bottom:3rem; opacity:0; transform:translateY(16px); transition:opacity 0.5s ease,transform 0.5s ease; scroll-margin-top:64px; }
      .doc-section.visible { opacity:1; transform:none; }
      .section-divider { background:var(--bg-divider); border:1px solid var(--border-bright); border-radius:2px; text-align:center; padding:1.5rem; margin-bottom:2rem; }
      .section-num { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.3em; color:var(--gold-dim); margin-bottom:0.4rem; display:block; }
      .section-title { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:23px; font-weight:700; color:var(--gold); }
      .h1 { font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-size:28px; font-weight:700; color:var(--gold); margin:2rem 0 0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.4rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); line-height:1.3; }
      .h2 { font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-size:22px; font-weight:600; color:var(--text-primary); margin:1.5rem 0 0.6rem; line-height:1.3; }
      p { margin-bottom:0.85rem; color:var(--text-primary); }
      .tags { margin:0.5rem 0 1.25rem; display:flex; gap:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap; }
      .tag { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:9.5px; letter-spacing:0.15em; color:var(--gold); background:var(--tag-bg); border:1px solid var(--border); padding:0.2rem 0.6rem; border-radius:2px; }
      .callout { background:var(--bg-card); border-left:4px solid var(--gold); padding:1.25rem 1.5rem; margin:1.5rem 0; font-size:18px; line-height:1.8; color:var(--text-primary); }
      .table-wrap { overflow-x:auto; margin:1rem 0; }
      table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:15px; }
      th { background:#0A2240; color:#B5D4F4; font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.12em; padding:0.6rem 0.8rem; text-align:left; border:1px solid rgba(21,63,106,0.4); font-weight:600; }
      td { padding:0.5rem 0.8rem; border:1px solid var(--border); color:var(--text-secondary); vertical-align:top; line-height:1.6; font-size:15px; }
      tr:nth-child(even) td { background:rgba(255,255,255,0.015); }
      tr:hover td { background:rgba(201,168,76,0.03); }
      .def-row { display:grid; grid-template-columns:180px 160px 1fr; gap:0; }
      .def-row > div { padding:0.6rem 0.8rem; border:1px solid var(--border); font-size:15.5px; }
      .def-row:first-child > div { background:#0A2240; color:#B5D4F4; font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:12px; letter-spacing:0.1em; font-weight:600; }
      .def-row:nth-child(even) > div { background:rgba(255,255,255,0.015); }
      .def-term { color:var(--gold); font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:14px; letter-spacing:0.08em; }
      .def-pron { color:var(--text-muted); }
      .def-def { color:var(--text-secondary); }
      .scripture-wrap { margin:1.25rem 0; }
      .scripture-toggle { width:100%; background:none; border:none; text-align:left; cursor:pointer; padding:0.6rem 0.9rem; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; gap:0.5rem; border-left:3px solid var(--gold-dim); background:rgba(201,168,76,0.04); transition:background 0.2s; }
      .scripture-toggle:hover { background:rgba(201,168,76,0.08); }
      .scripture-ref-label { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:14px; letter-spacing:0.1em; color:var(--gold); }
      .scripture-chevron { font-size:13px; color:var(--gold-dim); transition:transform 0.25s; flex-shrink:0; }
      .scripture-toggle.open .scripture-chevron { transform:rotate(180deg); }
      .scripture-body { display:none; background:var(--bg-scripture); border-left:3px solid var(--gold); padding:1rem 1.25rem; font-size:18px; line-height:1.85; color:var(--text-primary); margin-top:1px; }
      .scripture-body.open { display:block; }
      /* ENTRY CARDS */
      .entries-container { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.5rem; }
      .entry-card { border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:2px; overflow:hidden; scroll-margin-top:64px; }
      .entry-header { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; padding:1rem 1.25rem; cursor:pointer; background:var(--bg-card); transition:background 0.2s; gap:1rem; }
      .entry-header:hover { background:rgba(201,168,76,0.05); }
      .entry-header.open { background:rgba(201,168,76,0.07); border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); }
      .entry-num-title { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; }
      .entry-num { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:26px; color:var(--border-bright); min-width:44px; line-height:1; }
      .entry-title { font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-size:20px; font-weight:600; color:var(--text-primary); line-height:1.3; }
      .entry-ref-toggle { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex-shrink:0; }
      .entry-ref { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:12px; letter-spacing:0.08em; color:var(--gold-dim); }
      .entry-chevron { font-size:15px; color:var(--gold-dim); transition:transform 0.25s; }
      .entry-header.open .entry-chevron { transform:rotate(180deg); }
      .entry-body { display:none; padding:1.5rem 1.5rem 2rem; background:var(--bg-section); }
      .entry-body.open { display:block; }
      .scripture-block { background:var(--bg-scripture); border-left:4px solid var(--gold); padding:1rem 1.25rem; margin-bottom:1.5rem; }
      .scripture-label { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:13px; letter-spacing:0.12em; color:var(--gold); margin-bottom:0.5rem; }
      .scripture-text { font-size:18.5px; line-height:1.85; color:var(--text-primary); }
      .speech-annotation { background:rgba(27,94,94,0.07); border-left:3px solid var(--teal-accent); padding:0.7rem 1rem; margin-top:0.6rem; border-radius:0 2px 2px 0; }
      .speech-annotation-type { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:10px; letter-spacing:0.2em; color:#4AAFAF; margin-bottom:0.35rem; }
      .speech-annotation-text { font-size:16px; line-height:1.75; color:var(--text-secondary); }
      .subsection { margin:1.5rem 0; }
      .subsec-title { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.2em; color:var(--gold-dim); border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); padding-bottom:0.4rem; margin-bottom:1rem; }
      .phrase-block { margin-bottom:1.25rem; padding:0.9rem 1rem; background:var(--bg-card); border-left:3px solid var(--teal-accent); }
      .phrase-title { font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-size:17.5px; font-weight:600; color:var(--text-primary); margin-bottom:0.4rem; }
      .phrase-meaning { font-size:17px; color:var(--text-secondary); line-height:1.75; }
      .coverage-list { list-style:none; padding:0; }
      .coverage-list li { padding:0.3rem 0; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(201,168,76,0.08); color:var(--text-secondary); font-size:16.5px; }
      .coverage-list li::before { content:"→ "; color:var(--gold-dim); }
      .nations-section { background:rgba(139,32,32,0.05); border:1px solid rgba(139,32,32,0.2); padding:1.25rem; border-radius:2px; margin-top:1.5rem; }
      .nation-block { margin-bottom:1.25rem; padding-bottom:1rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border); }
      .nation-block:last-child { border-bottom:none; }
      .nation-name { font-family:'Cinzel',serif; font-size:13px; font-weight:600; color:var(--red-accent); letter-spacing:0.1em; margin-bottom:0.4rem; }
      .nations-note { color:var(--text-secondary); font-size:17px; border-left:3px solid var(--red-accent); padding:0.5rem 0.75rem; margin:0.5rem 0; background:var(--bg-card); }
      .scroll-top { position:fixed; bottom:2rem; right:2rem; background:var(--bg-card); border:1px solid var(--border-bright); color:var(--gold); width:40px; height:40px; border-radius:2px; cursor:pointer; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; font-size:18px; opacity:0; transition:opacity 0.3s; z-index:200; }
      .scroll-top.visible { opacity:1; }
      .scroll-top:hover { background:rgba(201,168,76,0.1); }
      @media (max-width:480px) {
      .layout { grid-template-columns:1fr; }
      .sidebar { display:none; }
      .main { padding:1.5rem 1rem 4rem; }
      .dossier-title { font-size:30px; }
      .entry-header { flex-direction:column; align-items:flex-start; }
      .def-row { grid-template-columns:1fr; }
      }
      .home-link{
      display:inline-flex;
      align-items:center;
      justify-content:center;
      padding:0.45rem 0.9rem;
      background:linear-gradient(180deg,#C9A84C,#8B6F00);
      color:#111009;
      text-decoration:none;
      font-family:'Cinzel',serif;
      font-size:12px;
      font-weight:700;
      letter-spacing:0.12em;
      border:1px solid rgba(201,168,76,0.5);
      border-radius:3px;
      transition:all .2s ease;
      white-space:nowrap;
      }
      .home-link:hover{
      background:linear-gradient(180deg,#E8C560,#C9A84C);
      transform:translateY(-1px);
      }
      .sidebar-toggle{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:36px;height:36px;background:transparent;border:1px solid rgba(201,168,76,.35);cursor:pointer;border-radius:3px;flex-shrink:0}
      .sidebar-toggle-bars,.sidebar-toggle-bars:before,.sidebar-toggle-bars:after{display:block;width:16px;height:2px;background:#C9A84C;content:"";position:relative}
      .sidebar-toggle-bars:before{position:absolute;top:-5px}
      .sidebar-toggle-bars:after{position:absolute;top:5px}
      .header-left{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:.5rem}
      </style>
      </head>
      <body>
      <header class="site-header">
      <div class="site-header-inner">
      <div class="header-left">
      <a href="../index.html" class="home-link">HOME</a>
      <button class="sidebar-toggle" aria-label="Menu">
      <span class="sidebar-toggle-bars"></span>
      </button>
      </div>
      <span class="site-title">DABARAYAM (DEUTERONOMY) 28 CURSES PROOF DOSSIER</span>
      <div class="progress-bar-wrap"><div class="progress-bar-fill" id="progressFill"></div></div>
      <span class="section-indicator" id="sectionIndicator">Introduction</span>
      </div>
      </header>
      <div class="layout">
      <nav class="sidebar" id="sidebar">
      <div class="nav-label">Navigate</div>
      <a class="nav-item active" onclick="scrollToAnchor('secIntro')" id="nav-secIntro">
      <span class="nav-num">INTRO</span>Language Restoration
      </a>
      <a class="nav-item" onclick="scrollToAnchor('secDisclaimer')" id="nav-secDisclaimer">
      <span class="nav-num">DISC</span>Disclaimer
      </a>
      <a class="nav-item" onclick="scrollToAnchor('secSpeech')" id="nav-secSpeech">
      <span class="nav-num">0B</span>Forms of Scriptural Speech
      </a>
      <div class="nav-divider"></div>
      <div class="nav-label">DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28</div>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-1')" id="nav-entry-1">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">01</span>CURSED IN CITY AND FIELD
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-2')" id="nav-entry-2">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">02</span>CURSED BASKET AND STORE
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-3')" id="nav-entry-3">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">03</span>CURSED FRUIT OF BODY AND LAND
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-4')" id="nav-entry-4">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">04</span>CURSED IN COMING IN AND GOING OUT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-5')" id="nav-entry-5">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">05</span>CONFUSION, REBUKE, AND FRUSTRATION
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-6')" id="nav-entry-6">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">06</span>PESTILENCE CLEAVING
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-7')" id="nav-entry-7">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">07</span>CONSUMPTION, FEVER, INFLAMMATION
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-8')" id="nav-entry-8">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">08</span>BOTCH, SCAB, ITCH
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-9')" id="nav-entry-9">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">09</span>MADNESS, BLINDNESS, ASTONISHMENT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-10')" id="nav-entry-10">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">10</span>WIFE, HOUSE, VINEYARD LOST
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-11')" id="nav-entry-11">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">11</span>LIVESTOCK TAKEN AND NOT RESTORED
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-12')" id="nav-entry-12">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">12</span>CHILDREN GIVEN TO ANOTHER PEOPLE
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-13')" id="nav-entry-13">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">13</span>LABOR CONSUMED BY OTHERS
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-14')" id="nav-entry-14">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">14</span>MADNESS FROM WHAT IS SEEN
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-15')" id="nav-entry-15">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">15</span>SORE BOTCH FROM FOOT TO HEAD
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-16')" id="nav-entry-16">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">16</span>REMOVAL INTO A FOREIGN NATION - GODS IMPOSED
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-17')" id="nav-entry-17">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">17</span>BYWORD, PROVERB, ASTONISHMENT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-18')" id="nav-entry-18">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">18</span>MUCH LABOR, LITTLE RETURN
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-19')" id="nav-entry-19">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">19</span>VINEYARDS, NO FRUIT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-20')" id="nav-entry-20">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">20</span>RESOURCES WITHOUT BENEFIT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-21')" id="nav-entry-21">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">21</span>CHILDREN NOT ENJOYED
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-22')" id="nav-entry-22">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">22</span>LAND AND OUTPUT CONSUMED
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-23')" id="nav-entry-23">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">23</span>STRANGER ABOVE, YOU BELOW
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-24')" id="nav-entry-24">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">24</span>LENDING REVERSAL
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-25')" id="nav-entry-25">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">25</span>CURSES PURSUE
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-26')" id="nav-entry-26">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">26</span>SIGNS AND WONDERS
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-27')" id="nav-entry-27">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">27</span>NO JOY IN SERVICE
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-28')" id="nav-entry-28">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">28</span>SERVING ENEMIES
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-29')" id="nav-entry-29">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">29</span>NATION FROM FAR
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-30')" id="nav-entry-30">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">30</span>FIERCE NATION
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-31')" id="nav-entry-31">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">31</span>RESOURCES CONSUMED
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-32')" id="nav-entry-32">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">32</span>SIEGE IN ALL GATES
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-33')" id="nav-entry-33">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">33</span>EXTREME DISTRESS
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-34')" id="nav-entry-34">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">34</span>LOSS OF AFFECTION
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-35')" id="nav-entry-35">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">35</span>NO SHARING UNDER SCARCITY
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-36')" id="nav-entry-36">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">36</span>WOMEN UNDER DISTRESS
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-37')" id="nav-entry-37">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">37</span>GREAT AND CONTINUING PLAGUES
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-38')" id="nav-entry-38">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">38</span>DISEASES RETURN AND REMAIN
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-39')" id="nav-entry-39">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">39</span>ALL SICKNESS AND PLAGUES
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-40')" id="nav-entry-40">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">40</span>FEW IN NUMBER
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-41')" id="nav-entry-41">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">41</span>REMOVED FROM LAND
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-42')" id="nav-entry-42">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">42</span>SCATTERED AMONG ALL PEOPLE
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-43')" id="nav-entry-43">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">43</span>NO REST AND FEAR
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-44')" id="nav-entry-44">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">44</span>FEAR DAY AND NIGHT
      </a>
      <a class="nav-sub entry-nav" onclick="scrollToAnchor('entry-45')" id="nav-entry-45">
      <span style="color:var(--gold-dim);margin-right:4px;">45</span>SHIPS INTO SLAVERY
      </a>
      </nav>
      <main class="main" id="mainContent">
      <div class="title-block">
      <h1 class="dossier-title">DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28<br>Curses Proof Dossier v5</h1>
      <div class="dossier-subtitle">400-Year Evidence - Verses 16-68</div>
      <div class="dossier-meta">
      <span class="meta-pill">1611 KJV THROUGHOUT</span>
      <span class="meta-pill">#PROPHECY #JUDGMENT #HISTORICAL</span>
      </div>
      </div>
      <!-- INTRO SECTION -->
      <section class="doc-section visible" id="secIntro">
      <div class="section-divider">
      <span class="section-num">INTRODUCTION</span>
      <div class="section-title">Paleo-Hebrew Language Restoration</div>
      </div>
      <!-- INTRO_ZONE_START -->
      <div class="h2">Project Position on Language Restoration</div>
      <p>This project follows a Paleo-Hebrew restoration approach rather than later Rabbinic or so-called modern Hebrew traditions. The project holds that the earliest Hebrew writing tradition was Paleo-Hebrew, and that the language structure found in the oldest ancient Hebrew writings (Semitic inscriptions) differs greatly from the system commonly called so-called modern Hebrew today.</p>
      <div class="h2">Restoration Principles</div>
      <div class="callout">
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">Paleo-Hebrew is a consonantal writing system</strong> — consonants only. No vowel letters. No vowel markings. This is the original, unaltered form of the language.</p>
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">Nikkud</strong> — the system of dots and marks used to represent vowel sounds — was invented by Masoretic scribes between the 6th and 10th centuries CE. It is a post-captivity addition that has no origin in and no connection to Paleo-Hebrew.</p>
      <p>The Masoretes are confederates in the ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83 conspiracy — their deliberate alteration of the language is part of the prophesied effort to erase the name and identity of YASHARA'AL(Israel) from remembrance.</p>
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">Matres lectionis</strong> — the later scribal practice of repurposing consonant letters to indicate vowel sounds — is similarly a product of that same conspiracy. It is not a feature of Paleo-Hebrew and does not apply to this restoration.</p>
      <p>The original glyph forms of Paleo-Hebrew are ancient Semitic letter shapes. The square script in common use today is a post-exilic development introduced as part of the same systematic effort to sever YASHARA'AL(Israel) from their original covenant language.</p>
      <p>The pronunciation of the original language was corrupted through centuries of captivity, foreign occupation, language mixing, and the deliberate religious traditions imposed by the scribal schools operating within the ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83 confederacy. This project restores pronunciation to the earliest recoverable Semitic form.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="h2">ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83:4&#8211;5 &#8212; The Conspiracy to Erase the Name</div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--red-accent);">
      <p>"They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation; that the name of YASHARA'AL(Israel) may be no more in remembrance. For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against you."</p>
      </div>
      <div class="h2">Restoration Comparison</div>
      <div class="table-wrap">
      <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Paleo-Hebrew Restoration</th><th>Later Traditions (Corrupted &amp; Deceptive)</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
      <tr><td>Ancient Semitic letter shapes (glyphs)</td><td>Square script used today</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Consonants only — no added vowels</td><td>Expanded vowel systems (Nikkud)</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Simplified "AH" vowel restoration</td><td>Later pronunciation traditions</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Early Semitic pronunciation flow</td><td>Rabbinic pronunciation systems</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Paleo-Hebrew name reconstruction</td><td>So-called modern Hebrew transliterations</td></tr>
      </tbody>
      </table>
      </div>
      <div class="h2">Name Restoration Table</div>
      <p>The names below have been changed or replaced in later religious systems. This project restores each name to its Paleo-Hebrew form. Transliterations are rejected in favor of direct Paleo-Hebrew reconstruction.</p>
      <div class="table-wrap">
      <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Corrupted Form (Psalm 83ers)</th><th>Paleo-Hebrew Rendering</th><th>Pronunciation</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
      <tr><td>LORD</td><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YAHAWAH</td><td>YA-HA-WAH</td></tr>
      <tr><td>God</td><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">ALAHAYAM</td><td>AH-LA-HA-YAM</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Jesus</td><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YAHAWASHA</td><td>YA-HA-WA-SHA</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Israel</td><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YASHARA'AL</td><td>YA-SHA-RA-AL</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Moses</td><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">MASHAH</td><td>MA-SHAH</td></tr>
      </tbody>
      </table>
      </div>
      <div class="h2">Why Modern Pronouns Are Used</div>
      <p>Restoring the true names of YAHAWAH and YAHAWASHA is the first priority of this dossier. The second is making those restored names — and the scriptures that carry them — easy to read and understand. That is why this project updates the older English wording found in the original 1611 King James Bible.</p>
      <div class="table-wrap">
      <table>
      <thead><tr><th>Older English</th><th>Modern English</th></tr></thead>
      <tbody>
      <tr><td>thee / thou</td><td>you</td></tr>
      <tr><td>thy</td><td>your</td></tr>
      <tr><td>speaketh</td><td>speaks</td></tr>
      <tr><td>hath</td><td>has</td></tr>
      </tbody>
      </table>
      </div>
      <div class="callout" style="margin-top:1.5rem;">
      <p><strong style="color:var(--text-muted);font-size:12px;letter-spacing:0.1em;">OLDER ENGLISH</strong><br>"Thou shalt love the Lord thy God."</p>
      <p style="margin-top:0.75rem;"><strong style="color:var(--gold);font-size:12px;letter-spacing:0.1em;">ACCURATE RENDERING</strong><br>"You shall love YAHAWAH (FATHER) your ALAHAYAM (God)."</p>
      <p style="margin-top:0.75rem;color:var(--text-secondary);font-size:17px;">The meaning remains the same. The doctrine remains the same. The command remains the same. Only the older English wording is updated to improve understanding for modern readers.</p>
      </div>
      <!-- INTRO_ZONE_END -->
      </section>
      <!-- DISCLAIMER SECTION -->
      <section class="doc-section" id="secDisclaimer">
      <div class="section-divider">
      <span class="section-num">DISCLAIMER</span>
      <div class="section-title">Statement of Purpose</div>
      </div>
      <!-- DISCLAIMER_ZONE_START -->
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--gold);text-align:center;padding:1.5rem 2rem;margin-bottom:2rem;">
      <p style="font-family:'Cinzel',serif;font-size:15px;letter-spacing:0.2em;color:var(--gold);margin:0;">SHALAWAM (Peace) &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; MASPACHA (Family)</p>
      </div>
      <p>All praise, honor, and glory belong to <strong style="color:var(--gold);">YAHAWAH (FATHER)</strong>, the Eternal One, who has called us out of darkness into His marvelous light and restored our covenant understanding in this season of the Awakening.</p>
      <p>This dossier is a sincere effort from a Hebrew Israelite to restore covenant understanding and divine names based on verified historical sources and the 1611 King James Version of the Holy Bible. However, I acknowledge that we are all <strong>STILL LEARNING</strong>. Everything presented in this dossier requires prayer for discernment, personal verification, and righteous dialog in the search for truth.</p>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--red-accent);">
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">NOTE:</strong> WE MUST REMEMBER THAT THE CURSE WAS LIFTED IN 2019 BUT THAT DOES NOT MEAN THE EFFECTS AND THE SYSTEMS OF THE CURSES STOP FUNCTIONING. THEY HAVEN'T AND WE MUST BE DILIGENT TO ENDURE UNTIL THE END!</p>
      </div>
      <p>This dossier is by no means fully exhaustive but does provide a solid foundation to build from. I pray that this work is accepted as such.</p>
      <div class="h2">PRAY FOR DISCERNMENT OVER ALL THINGS</div>
      <p>Before engaging with this dossier, we earnestly encourage you to PRAY for discernment over everything presented. Do not accept any teaching blindly, even this one. Scripture instructs us clearly:</p>
      <div class="scripture-wrap">
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
      <p>"Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of ALAHAYAM (God): because many false prophets are gone out into the world."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; YAWACHANAN (1 John) 4:1 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
      <p>"Prove all things; hold fast that which is good."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; 1 Thessalonians 5:21 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:0.75rem;">
      <p>"For the word of ALAHAYAM (God) is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; Hebrews 4:12 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);">
      <p>"If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of ALAHAYAM (God), that gives to all men liberally, and upbraids not; and it shall be given him."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; James 1:5 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="h2">We Are Open to Righteous Dialog on the Search for Truth</div>
      <p>We are not closed to correction or alternate understanding. We welcome righteous dialog on the search for truth. If you have questions, corrections, alternative verified sources, or scriptural challenges to what is presented, bring them forward. Scripture teaches that truth comes through the sharpening of iron against iron:</p>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);">
      <p>"Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens the countenance of his friend."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; Proverbs 27:17 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <p>We do <strong>NOT</strong> operate from a place of claiming exclusive truth or demanding blind obedience. We explicitly reject:</p>
      <ul class="coverage-list" style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
      <li>Claims of "exclusive knowledge" that only we possess</li>
      <li>Demands for blind obedience to human leaders</li>
      <li>False tribal assignments designed to create hierarchy</li>
      <li>Any system that places camp leaders or teachers between you and YAHAWAH (FATHER)</li>
      </ul>
      <div class="h2">To the Hebrew Israelites and Strangers That Cleave</div>
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">TO THE HEBREW ISRAELITES:</strong> This guide is intended to help you remember who you are and whose you are. You are the covenant people, chosen and called by YAHAWAH (FATHER). Search diligently for truth. Do not rest until you have verified every claim against scripture and verified historical sources.</p>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:1rem;">
      <p>"Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; Matthew 5:6 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <p><strong style="color:var(--gold);">TO THE STRANGERS THAT CLEAVE:</strong> Scripture recognizes that there are those who are not naturally born into the covenant but who turn their hearts toward YAHAWAH (FATHER) and His Law. You are welcome. One Law applies to all who enter into covenant:</p>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:1rem;">
      <p>"One law shall be to him that is homeborn, and unto the stranger that sojourns among you."<br><span style="color:var(--gold-dim);font-size:15px;">&#8212; SHAMAWATH (Exodus) 12:49 (KJV)</span></p>
      </div>
      <div class="h2" style="text-align:center;border-bottom:none;">YAHAWAH (FATHER) IS READY TO HEAR US NOW</div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--gold);text-align:center;margin-top:1rem;">
      <p>This is the season of the Awakening. YAHAWAH (FATHER) is calling His people back to covenant obedience and covenant understanding. The door is open. He is ready to hear us NOW.</p>
      </div>
      <!-- DISCLAIMER_ZONE_END -->
      </section>
      <!-- FORMS OF SCRIPTURAL SPEECH SECTION -->
      <section class="doc-section" id="secSpeech">
      <div class="section-divider">
      <span class="section-num">SECTION 0B</span>
      <div class="section-title">Forms of Scriptural Speech</div>
      </div>
      <div class="callout" style="border-left-color:var(--teal-accent);margin-bottom:2rem;">
      <p>Scripture does not always speak in plain language. YAHAWAH(FATHER) uses metaphor, symbol, allegory, vision, and prophecy to communicate truth that transcends what plain language can carry. This section defines the forms of speech used throughout this dossier. Wherever one of these devices appears in a scripture block, a teal annotation box below the verse will identify the device and explain what it is doing in that specific verse. The reader never has to leave the page.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="subsec-title">FIGURATIVE COMPARISONS</div>
      <div class="entries-container">
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-metaphor">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">METAPHOR</span>
      <span class="entry-title">A Direct Comparison Without &ldquo;Like&rdquo; or &ldquo;As&rdquo;</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>A metaphor states that one thing <em>is</em> another, without using a comparing word. It does not say something is <em>like</em> something - it declares it directly. In scripture, metaphors compress large spiritual truths into vivid concrete images.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28:48</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">He shall put a yoke of iron upon your neck, until he have destroyed you.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">METAPHOR IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The &ldquo;yoke of iron&rdquo; is a metaphor for total subjugation and control. Historically, iron slave collars and chains were placed literally on the necks and bodies of enslaved Israelites - making this both a metaphor and a literal fulfillment in the Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade. The yoke carried by cattle becomes the image of the covenant people under captivity.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-simile">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">SIMILE</span>
      <span class="entry-title">A Comparison Using &ldquo;Like&rdquo; or &ldquo;As&rdquo;</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>A simile makes an explicit comparison between two different things using the words <em>like</em> or <em>as</em>. The comparing word signals that the meaning is comparative, not literal. Similes in scripture build vivid pictures that make abstract truths immediately felt.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">ZAMAYAR (Psalms) 44:22</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">We are counted as sheep for the slaughter.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">SIMILE IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The covenant people are compared to sheep led to slaughter - passive, numerous, and expendable in the eyes of the oppressor system. The simile captures powerlessness under state violence. The Apostle Shaul (Paul) quotes this exact verse in Romans 8:36 regarding the ongoing persecution of covenant people.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-allegory">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">ALLEGORY</span>
      <span class="entry-title">An Extended Story Where Every Element Has a Deeper Meaning</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>An allegory is a narrative where the surface story and all its characters, events, and objects represent another layer of spiritual or historical truth. Unlike a metaphor (a single image) or a parable (a brief story with one main lesson), an allegory is systematic - every element carries meaning.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YACHAZAQA&rsquo;AL (Ezekiel) 37:3-5</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">Can these bones live? &hellip; Prophesy upon these bones &hellip; I will cause breath to enter into you, and you shall live.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">ALLEGORY IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The Valley of Dry Bones is a complete allegory: the bones = the scattered nation; the valley = captivity and exile; the dryness = spiritual death; the breath = YAHAWAH&rsquo;s Spirit (RAWACH QADASH); the standing army = a restored covenant nation. No element is literal - every element points to the national and covenantal restoration of YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel).</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div><!-- end entries-container -->
      <div class="subsec-title" style="margin-top:2rem;">SYMBOLIC &amp; PROPHETIC LANGUAGE</div>
      <div class="entries-container">
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-symbol">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">SYMBOL</span>
      <span class="entry-title">An Object or Image That Stands for Something Greater</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>A symbol is a concrete object, image, name, or action that represents a larger truth, identity, or covenant reality. Symbols in scripture are consistent - the same symbol carries the same meaning across books and centuries.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28:68</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">And YAHAWAH shall bring you into Egypt again with ships.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">SYMBOL IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">&ldquo;Egypt&rdquo; in this verse is not the literal nation - it is a prophetic symbol for any place of forced bondage and captivity. The ships in this verse were literally fulfilled in the Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade: YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) was carried by ship into a new Egypt - America. The symbol of Egypt for bondage runs from BARA&rsquo;AHSHAYATH(Genesis) through Revelation.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-sign">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">SIGN</span>
      <span class="entry-title">A Visible Covenant Marker Pointing to a Spiritual Truth</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>A sign is a physical, observable event or object that YAHAWAH(FATHER) appoints to mark a covenant truth or promise. Unlike a symbol (which can be textual), a sign is something witnessed - a rainbow, a name, a plague, a blood mark. Signs confirm covenant reality in the visible world.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">HAWASHA (Hosea) 1:9</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">Call his name Loammi: for you are not my people, and I will not be your ALAHAYAM(God).</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">SIGN IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The name &ldquo;Loammi&rdquo; (meaning &ldquo;not my people&rdquo;) is a covenantal sign - YAHAWAH commands the prophet to give his child this name as a visible public marker of the covenant&rsquo;s broken condition. It is a sign of judgment, not permanent rejection: HAWASHA(Hosea) 1:10 immediately follows with a promise of restoration.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-typeantitype">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">TYPE / ANTITYPE</span>
      <span class="entry-title">A Person or Event That Previews a Future Fulfillment</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>A <strong>type</strong> is a person, event, or institution in scripture that prefigures (previews) something greater to come. The <strong>antitype</strong> is the fulfillment - the greater reality that the type pointed toward. Types are forward-looking; antitypes are the arrival. YAHAWAH embeds typological patterns across centuries of scripture.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">BARA&rsquo;AHSHAYATH (Genesis) 15:13-14</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">Know of a surety that your seed shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs, and shall serve them; and they shall afflict them four hundred years.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">TYPE / ANTITYPE IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The first 400-year captivity in Egypt is the TYPE - the preview. The Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade captivity (1619-2019) is the ANTITYPE - the greater fulfillment. The pattern is the same: foreign land, affliction, a set time, judgment of the oppressor nation, and deliverance with great substance.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-dualfulfillment">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">DUAL FULFILLMENT</span>
      <span class="entry-title">A Prophecy With Both a Near and a Far Fulfillment</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>Many prophecies in scripture have two fulfillments: one that occurred relatively soon after the prophecy was given (near fulfillment), and one that occurs in a later era at a greater scale (far fulfillment). The near fulfillment confirms the prophet; the far fulfillment reveals the full scope of YAHAWAH&rsquo;s plan.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YAWACHANAN (Luke) 21:24</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">They shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">DUAL FULFILLMENT IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">Near fulfillment: 70 AD - Rome destroys Jerusalem, slaughters over a million Israelites, leads survivors captive. Far fulfillment: the Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade scatters YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) into all nations worldwide - the greatest scattering in human history. Both are confirmed historically.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-visionlanguage">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">VISION LANGUAGE</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Symbolic Imagery Seen in Prophetic Visions</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>When prophets receive visions, they describe what they see in images - horses, beasts, numbers, colors, flames, scrolls. These are not literal photographs; they are symbolic images that must be interpreted. Vision language requires identifying what each element represents rather than reading it as physical description.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">Revelation 1:14-15</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">His head and his hairs were white like wool, as white as snow; and his eyes were as a flame of fire; and his feet like unto fine brass, as if they burned in a furnace.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">VISION LANGUAGE IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">This is YAWACHANAN&rsquo;s vision of YAHAWASHA(MESSIAH). Each image is symbolic: &ldquo;hair like wool&rdquo; describes texture consistent with an elder of Semitic descent; &ldquo;eyes as a flame of fire&rdquo; means all-seeing judgment; &ldquo;feet like burned brass&rdquo; describes dark, refined, strong - the appearance of the risen YAHAWASHA. This is not the European image promoted during slavery.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div><!-- end entries-container -->
      <div class="subsec-title" style="margin-top:2rem;">RHETORICAL DEVICES</div>
      <div class="entries-container">
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-personification">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">PERSONIFICATION</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Non-Human Things Given Human Attributes</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>Personification gives human characteristics - feelings, speech, actions - to non-human things: nations, cities, nature, abstract forces. In Hebrew scripture, nations and cities are regularly personified as women to convey vulnerability, grief, and displacement. This is a literary device, not a gender statement.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YARAMAYAH (Lamentations) 1:3</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">YASHARA&rsquo;AL is gone into captivity&hellip; she dwells among the heathen, she finds no rest: all her persecutors overtook her between the straits.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">PERSONIFICATION IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">The nation YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) is personified as a woman - &ldquo;she dwells,&rdquo; &ldquo;she finds no rest,&rdquo; &ldquo;her persecutors.&rdquo; This is a classic Hebrew poetic device: nations and cities are given female personhood to convey vulnerability, grief, and displacement. The personification makes the suffering of millions intimate and immediate.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-hyperbole">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">HYPERBOLE</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Deliberate Overstatement for Emphasis</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>Hyperbole is exaggerated language used not to deceive, but to drive home the emotional weight or extreme nature of a situation. It is not a lie - it is an intentional overstatement meant to be felt, not measured. Scripture uses hyperbole to convey the depth of sin, the intensity of judgment, and the magnitude of blessing.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YASHAYAH (Isaiah) 9:19-20</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">They shall eat every man the flesh of his own arm: Manasseh, Ephraim; and Ephraim, Manasseh.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">HYPERBOLE IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">&ldquo;Eat the flesh of his own arm&rdquo; is not a literal description of cannibalism - it is a hyperbolic image for extreme self-destruction. The overstatement conveys that the internal violence of YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) has reached its absolute extreme: consuming itself. Gang violence, fratricide, and intra-community destruction are the modern fulfillment of this prophetic hyperbole.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-irony">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">IRONY</span>
      <span class="entry-title">When the Stated Meaning Is the Opposite of the True Meaning</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>Irony occurs when words carry a meaning opposite to their surface reading, or when a situation is the reverse of what is expected. <strong>Dramatic irony</strong> is when the audience knows something the speaker does not. Scripture uses irony to expose self-deception, highlight spiritual blindness, and frame events as covenant commentary.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YAWACHANAN (John) 8:33</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">We be Abraham&rsquo;s seed, and were never in bondage to any man.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">DRAMATIC IRONY IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">This claim is made while the speakers live under Roman military occupation - a form of bondage visible to every reader. The audience knows they are in bondage even as they deny it. It directly mirrors the modern claim among YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) that civil rights legislation or financial success constitutes true freedom. YAHAWASHA(MESSIAH) corrects both denials immediately.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-idiom">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">IDIOM</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Expressions Whose Literal Meaning Differs From Intended Meaning</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>An idiom is an expression whose meaning cannot be derived from the literal meanings of its individual words. Every language has idioms that speakers understand by convention, not by reading the words directly. Hebrew scripture contains many idioms that must be understood in their cultural and linguistic context, not read literally.</p>
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">YARAMAYAH (Lamentations) 5:5-6</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">We have given the hand to the Egyptians, and to the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation">
      <div class="speech-annotation-type">IDIOM IN USE</div>
      <div class="speech-annotation-text">&ldquo;Given the hand&rdquo; is a Hebrew idiom meaning to submit, surrender, or enter subjugation under a foreign power - not a literal handshake. YASHARA&rsquo;AL(Israel) was forced into conditions of servitude and dependence under captors just to survive. The idiom conveys the depth of covenant people&rsquo;s subjugation: reduced to dependence on their oppressors for basic bread.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div><!-- end entries-container -->
      <div class="subsec-title" style="margin-top:2rem;">NUMBER, COLOR &amp; NAME SYMBOLISM</div>
      <div class="entries-container">
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-numbersymbolism">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">NUMBER SYMBOLISM</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Numbers That Carry Covenant Meaning Beyond Their Count</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>In scripture, numbers are not always just quantities - they carry established covenant meanings. These meanings are consistent across the entire biblical record and must be understood to read prophetic passages accurately.</p>
      <div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>NUMBER</th><th>COVENANT MEANING</th><th>SCRIPTURE EXAMPLE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>400</td><td>A set period of captivity and trial</td><td>BARA&rsquo;AHSHAYATH 15:13 - 400 years in Egypt; 1619-2019 Trans-Atlantic captivity</td></tr>
      <tr><td>40</td><td>A period of testing and trial</td><td>40 years in the wilderness; 40 days of YAHAWASHA&rsquo;s testing</td></tr>
      <tr><td>7</td><td>Completion and covenant fullness</td><td>7 days of creation; 7th day Sabbath; 7 assemblies of Revelation</td></tr>
      <tr><td>12</td><td>The tribes of YASHARA&rsquo;AL - covenant completeness</td><td>12 tribes; 12 apostles; 144,000 (12 &times; 12 &times; 1,000)</td></tr>
      <tr><td>3</td><td>The witness standard - established truth</td><td>DABARAYAM 17:6 - two or three witnesses establish a matter</td></tr>
      <tr><td>70</td><td>Nations and the fullness of the Gentile age</td><td>70 nations in BARA&rsquo;AHSHAYATH 10; 70 years of Babylonian captivity; 70 AD Roman destruction</td></tr>
      </table></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-colorsymbolism">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">COLOR SYMBOLISM</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Colors That Carry Spiritual and Covenant Meaning</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>Colors in scripture carry established spiritual and covenant meanings. When they appear in vision language, in descriptions of the Tabernacle, in prophetic imagery, or in descriptions of persons and events, they are not decorative - they are communicating covenant truth.</p>
      <div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>COLOR</th><th>COVENANT MEANING</th><th>SCRIPTURE EXAMPLE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Scarlet / Red</td><td>Sin, blood, sacrifice, redemption</td><td>YASHAYAH 1:18 - though your sins be as scarlet; Revelation 19 - vesture dipped in blood</td></tr>
      <tr><td>White</td><td>Righteousness, purity, victory</td><td>Revelation 19:8 - fine linen, white and clean = righteousness of the set-apart</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Purple</td><td>Royalty, kingship, authority</td><td>Used in the Tabernacle curtains - the presence of YAHAWAH</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Black</td><td>Affliction, mourning, judgment in prophetic context</td><td>Lamentations 4:8 - visage blacker than coal (affliction under captivity)</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Gold / Brass</td><td>Refined, tested, covenant strength</td><td>Revelation 1:15 - feet like burned brass - strength refined through suffering</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Blue</td><td>The commandments and the covenant law</td><td>Numbers 15:38 - blue thread to remember the commandments</td></tr>
      </table></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="speech-namesymbolism">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--teal-accent);min-width:unset;padding-right:0.5rem;">NAME SYMBOLISM</span>
      <span class="entry-title">Names as Declarations of Covenant Identity and Purpose</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle"><span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span></div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <p>In Paleo-Hebrew scripture, names are not labels - they are declarations. A name announces identity, purpose, covenant status, and divine intention. When YAHAWAH(FATHER) changes a name, or commands a name to be given, it is a covenant act - not a cultural convenience.</p>
      <div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>NAME</th><th>COVENANT DECLARATION</th></tr>
      <tr><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YAHAWAH</td><td>The self-existing, eternal one - He who was, is, and will be. Not a title. A covenant name. Replaced approximately 6,828 times with &ldquo;LORD&rdquo; in translations.</td></tr>
      <tr><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YAHAWASHA</td><td>YAHAWAH saves / YAHAWAH is salvation. The MESSIAH carries the FATHER&rsquo;s name within His own. &ldquo;Jesus&rdquo; carries no such covenant meaning.</td></tr>
      <tr><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">YASHARA&rsquo;AL</td><td>One who prevails with ALAHAYAM. The covenant name of the chosen people - not a geographic nation.</td></tr>
      <tr><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">ABARAHAM</td><td>Father of many nations - a covenant name change from Abram (exalted father) to ABARAHAM (father of multitudes).</td></tr>
      <tr><td style="color:var(--gold);font-family:'Cinzel',serif;letter-spacing:0.05em;">Loammi</td><td>&ldquo;Not my people&rdquo; - a name-sign of covenant judgment given temporarily; reversed in HAWASHA(Hosea) 1:10.</td></tr>
      </table></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      </div><!-- end entries-container -->
      </section>
      <!-- END FORMS OF SCRIPTURAL SPEECH SECTION -->
      <!-- 45 CURSES SECTION -->
      <section class="doc-section visible" id="secCurses">
      <div class="section-divider">
      <span class="section-num">DABARAYAM (Deuteronomy) 28:16-68</span>
      <div class="section-title">400-YEAR EVIDENCE - VERSES 16-68</div>
      </div>
      <div class="tags"><span class="tag">#PROPHECY</span><span class="tag">#JUDGMENT</span><span class="tag">#HISTORICAL</span></div>
      <div class="entries-container">
      <!-- ENTRIES_ZONE_START -->
      <div class="entry-card" id="entry-1">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num">01</span>
      <span class="entry-title">CURSED IN CITY AND FIELD</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle">
      <span class="entry-ref">DABARAYAM(Deuteronomy) 28:16</span>
      <span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">DABARAYAM(Deuteronomy) 28:16</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">Cursed will you be in the city, and cursed will you be in the field.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">WHAT THIS VERSE MEANS - PHRASE BY PHRASE</div>
      <div class="phrase-block">
      <div class="phrase-title">&ldquo;Cursed will you be in the city&rdquo;</div>
      <div class="phrase-meaning"><strong>This means:</strong> No matter where YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) works or lives in an urban area - the jobs, housing, and businesses - everything is under an active curse working against them. Not bad luck. A specific judgment.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="phrase-block">
      <div class="phrase-title">&ldquo;Cursed will you be in the field&rdquo;</div>
      <div class="phrase-meaning"><strong>This means:</strong> Every place YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) does labor outside the city - farming, agriculture, manual work - is also under the same curse. Both environments are covered simultaneously. There is no safe location.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">MODERN EXPLANATION</div><p>This curse establishes that YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) (Hebrew Israelites) will experience hardship in every environment, not just one. There is no &quot;safe place&quot; condition: not in labor systems (field), not in populated systems (city). The condition is continuous, not temporary. It transitions across time but does not stop.</p></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">REAL-WORLD EVIDENCE - UNITED STATES (400-YEAR COVERAGE)</div><div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>EVENT</th><th>DATE</th><th>LOCATION</th><th>MATCH TO CURSE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Chattel Slavery Agricultural Labor</td><td>1619-1865</td><td>United States</td><td>Field is cursed - labor produces no benefit for the worker.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Sharecropping System</td><td>1865-1930s</td><td>Southern United States</td><td>Field remains cursed - labor continues without gain.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Great Migration Urban Conditions</td><td>1916-1970</td><td>Chicago, Detroit, New York</td><td>City becomes cursed - environment shifts but condition remains.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Gang-Controlled Urban Environments</td><td>1970s-Today</td><td>Chicago, Los Angeles, Baltimore</td><td>City remains cursed - persistent instability.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Mass Incarceration System</td><td>1970s-Today</td><td>United States</td><td>No escape condition - both city and field environments produce hardship.</td></tr>
      </table></div></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">COVERAGE VALIDATION - UNITED STATES</div><ul class="coverage-list"><li>1600s-1800s -&gt; slavery (field)</li><li>1800s-1900s -&gt; sharecropping (field)</li><li>1900s -&gt; migration (city)</li><li>late 1900s-Today-&gt; gang environments (city)</li><li>modern -&gt; incarceration (cross-environment)</li></ul></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">DIASPORA WITNESS - GLOBAL FULFILLMENT</div><div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>EVENT</th><th>DATE</th><th>LOCATION</th><th>MATCH TO CURSE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Sugar Plantation Labor - enslaved in city and field</td><td>1640-1838</td><td>Barbados, Jamaica, Trinidad</td><td>Field cursed - forced labor with no gain in all environments.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Favela Urban Confinement</td><td>1900s-Today</td><td>Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo, Brazil</td><td>City cursed - urban poverty with no escape condition.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Colonial Field Labor under Dutch Control</td><td>1650-1863</td><td>Suriname, Guyana</td><td>Field cursed - plantation labor produces nothing for the worker.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Post-independence Urban Slum Conditions</td><td>1804-Today</td><td>Port-au-Prince, Haiti</td><td>City cursed - persistent urban instability across generations.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Banana Plantation Labor Exploitation</td><td>1900s-1960s</td><td>Honduras, Belize, Panama</td><td>Field cursed - corporate-controlled land yields no benefit.</td></tr>
      </table></div></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">DIASPORA COVERAGE VALIDATION</div><ul class="coverage-list"><li>1640-1838 -&gt; Caribbean plantation field labor</li><li>1650-1863 -&gt; Dutch colonial field systems (Suriname, Guyana)</li><li>1804-Today-&gt; Haitian urban poverty</li><li>1900s-Today-&gt; Brazilian favela city conditions</li><li>1900s-1960s -&gt; Central American plantation exploitation</li></ul></div>
      <div class="subsection nations-section"><div class="subsec-title">NATIONS OF THE CONSPIRACY - ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83 ACTORS</div><p class="nations-note">Portugal</p><p class="nations-note">The Portuguese established the plantation field labor template in Brazil beginning in the 1500s, making Brazil the single largest recipient of enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) - an estimated 4 to 5 million people.</p><p class="nations-note">Dutch West India Company</p><p class="nations-note">The Dutch West India Company (WIC) replicated and expanded this system across Suriname, Curaçao, and their Caribbean holdings, operating plantation field labor under codified colonial law.</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish Merchants and Plantation Owners</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish merchant families - including the Gradis family of Bordeaux and Sephardic planters in Suriname - owned sugar plantations and held enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) as field workers, representing some of the highest per-capita slave ownership of any demographic group in the Americas.</p><p class="nations-note">Britain - Royal African Company</p><p class="nations-note">The British Royal African Company, chartered by the British crown, transported enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) into plantation field systems across Jamaica, Barbados, and the American colonies.</p><p class="nations-note">France</p><p class="nations-note">The French under the Code Noir (1685) formalized field slavery in Martinique, Guadeloupe, and Saint-Domingue (Haiti).</p><p class="nations-note">Spain</p><p class="nations-note">Spanish colonial law through the Asiento system licensed the field labor system across Cuba, Puerto Rico, Colombia, and Panama.</p><p class="nations-note">Dahomey, Ashanti, and Oyo Kingdoms</p><p class="nations-note">The Dahomey kingdom of West Africa, the Ashanti of Ghana, and the Oyo Empire of Nigeria supplied captives to these systems, functioning as internal actors in the conspiracy.</p><p class="nations-note">Not one of these nations is excused.</p><p class="nations-note">Every actor named above is a fulfillment of ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83 - the confederacy working to keep YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) cursed in city and in field.</p></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="entry-2">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num">02</span>
      <span class="entry-title">CURSED BASKET AND STORE</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle">
      <span class="entry-ref">DABARAYAM(Deuteronomy) 28:17</span>
      <span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <div class="scripture-block">
      <div class="scripture-label">DABARAYAM(Deuteronomy) 28:17</div>
      <div class="scripture-text">Cursed will be your basket and your store.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">WHAT THIS VERSE MEANS - PHRASE BY PHRASE</div>
      <div class="phrase-block">
      <div class="phrase-title">&ldquo;Cursed will be your basket&rdquo;</div>
      <div class="phrase-meaning"><strong>This means:</strong> The basket represents daily provisions - food, household goods, everyday needs. Under this curse, whatever goes into the basket costs more and runs out faster. Daily survival is made unstable by design.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="phrase-block">
      <div class="phrase-title">&ldquo;Cursed will be your store&rdquo;</div>
      <div class="phrase-meaning"><strong>This means:</strong> The store is what is set aside for later - savings, reserves, wealth. Under this curse, savings disappear before they can grow. There is never enough accumulated to get ahead, regardless of how hard YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) works.</div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">MODERN EXPLANATION</div><p>This curse establishes failure at both levels of provision for YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) (Hebrew Israelites). Daily survival (basket) is unstable, and long-term accumulation (store) is either prevented or destroyed. Across the 400-year span, YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) experience both lack of consistent daily provision and loss or blockage of generational wealth.</p></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">REAL-WORLD EVIDENCE - UNITED STATES (400-YEAR COVERAGE)</div><div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>EVENT</th><th>DATE</th><th>LOCATION</th><th>MATCH TO CURSE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Enslavement without wages</td><td>1619-1865</td><td>United States</td><td>No basket control and no store exists.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Freedman&#39;s Savings Bank collapse</td><td>1874</td><td>United States</td><td>Store destroyed after accumulation.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Redlining and credit denial</td><td>1930s-1968</td><td>United States</td><td>Store prevented from forming.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Deindustrialization job loss</td><td>1970s-1990s</td><td>Detroit, Chicago, Cleveland</td><td>Basket destabilized.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Housing crisis and foreclosures</td><td>2008</td><td>United States</td><td>Basket disrupted and store wiped out.</td></tr>
      </table></div></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">COVERAGE VALIDATION - UNITED STATES</div><ul class="coverage-list"><li>1600s-1800s -&gt; no wages (no basket/store)</li><li>1800s -&gt; savings loss</li><li>1900s -&gt; blocked wealth systems</li><li>Late 1900s -&gt; income instability</li><li>Modern -&gt; wealth destruction</li></ul></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">DIASPORA WITNESS - GLOBAL FULFILLMENT</div><div class="table-wrap"><table>
      <tr><th>EVENT</th><th>DATE</th><th>LOCATION</th><th>MATCH TO CURSE</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Emancipation without land or compensation</td><td>1838</td><td>Jamaica, Barbados, Trinidad</td><td>No basket and no store - freed with nothing.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Post-abolition debt peonage</td><td>1888-1920s</td><td>Brazil</td><td>Store blocked - debt systems replace wages.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Haiti forced reparations to France</td><td>1825-1947</td><td>Haiti</td><td>Store extracted - national wealth drained for 122 years.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Denial of land ownership post-emancipation</td><td>1834-1900s</td><td>Guyana, Suriname</td><td>No basket base - no land means no food security.</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Colonial currency control and import dependency</td><td>1900s-1960s</td><td>Nigeria, Ghana, Sierra Leone</td><td>Basket and store controlled by foreign systems.</td></tr>
      </table></div></div>
      <div class="subsection"><div class="subsec-title">DIASPORA COVERAGE VALIDATION</div><ul class="coverage-list"><li>1825-1947 -&gt; Haiti reparation debt to France</li><li>1838 -&gt; Caribbean emancipation with no land or wages</li><li>1888-1920s -&gt; Brazilian debt peonage</li><li>1834-1900s -&gt; Guyana/Suriname land denial</li><li>1900s-1960s -&gt; West African colonial economic control</li></ul></div>
      <div class="subsection nations-section"><div class="subsec-title">NATIONS OF THE CONSPIRACY - ZAMAYAR(Psalms) 83 ACTORS</div><p class="nations-note">CONTEXT</p><p class="nations-note">The destruction of the basket and store of YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) was a coordinated act carried out by multiple nations.</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish Merchants and Plantation Owners</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish banking and merchant families - including Aaron Lopez of Newport, Rhode Island, one of the most documented slave traders in American history, and the Sephardic merchant networks of Amsterdam and Recife - served as financiers and commercial brokers, ensuring that the economic output of enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) flowed into European and Jewish merchant wealth.</p><p class="nations-note">Genoese and Venetian Banking Houses</p><p class="nations-note">The Genoese and Venetian banking houses of Italy provided the original capital that financed Portuguese slave voyages, functioning as the commercial engine of the Transatlantic trade.</p><p class="nations-note">France extracted 150 million francs in reparations from Haiti after the 1804 revolution - a debt that took 122 years to repay, draining Haiti&#39;s national store for over a century.</p><p class="nations-note">Britain</p><p class="nations-note">The British facilitated the collapse of the Freedman&#39;s Savings Bank in 1874, wiping out accumulated savings of formerly enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) .</p><p class="nations-note">The Netherlands - Dutch</p><p class="nations-note">The Dutch colonial system in Suriname and Curaçao ensured that no basket or store could form in those territories.</p><p class="nations-note">Vatican</p><p class="nations-note">The Vatican through its Papal Bulls - Dum Diversas (1452) and Romanus Pontifex (1455) - provided the legal and spiritual authorization for the entire economic system that destroyed the basket and store of YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) .</p><p class="nations-note">All these actors together fulfilled this curse.</p></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-card" id="entry-3">
      <div class="entry-header" onclick="toggleEntry(this)">
      <div class="entry-num-title">
      <span class="entry-num">03</span>
      <span class="entry-title">CURSED FRUIT OF BODY AND LAND</span>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-ref-toggle">
      <span class="entry-ref">DABARAYAM(Deuteronomy) 28:18</span>
      <span class="entry-chevron">&#9660;</span>
      </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entry-body">
      <div class="scripture-block">
