Royal African Company</p><p class="nations-note">The British Royal African Company - chartered by King Charles II - became the largest single slave trading enterprise in history, transporting an estimated 100,000 enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) .</p><p class="nations-note">Dutch West India Company</p><p class="nations-note">The Dutch West India Company transported enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) across the Atlantic as a core commercial activity, with Curaçao serving as a major transit depot.</p><p class="nations-note">France</p><p class="nations-note">The French transported enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) into Saint-Domingue, Martinique, and Guadeloupe through state-sanctioned commercial enterprises.</p><p class="nations-note">Danish West India Company</p><p class="nations-note">The Danish West India Company transported enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) into the Virgin Islands by ship.</p><p class="nations-note">Sweden - Swedish South Company</p><p class="nations-note">The Swedish South Company participated in the West African coast trade.</p><p class="nations-note">Brandenburg/Prussia - Brandenburger African Company</p><p class="nations-note">The Brandenburg African Company of Prussia operated slave forts on the Gold Coast and participated in transportation.</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish Merchants and Plantation Owners</p><p class="nations-note">Jewish ship owners and investors - whose names are documented in the records of the time - include Aaron Lopez of Newport, Rhode Island, one of the most documented individual slave ship investors in American history; the Sephardic merchant families of Amsterdam who invested in Dutch WIC slave voyages; and the documented Jewish plantation owners of Suriname and Brazil who received transported YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) as their commercial property.</p><p class="nations-note">Arab Slave Traders - Ishmaelites</p><p class="nations-note">The Arab dhow trade transported enslaved YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) across the Indian Ocean and the Red Sea for over a thousand years.</p><p class="nations-note">Dahomey, Ashanti, and Oyo Kingdoms</p><p class="nations-note">The Dahomey, Ashanti, and Oyo kingdoms delivered YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) to the shores and the ships.</p><p class="nations-note">Not one ship.</p><p class="nations-note">Not one nation.</p><p class="nations-note">Not one merchant house.</p><p class="nations-note">Not one name that history has recorded will be omitted from this accounting.</p><p class="nations-note">YAHAWAH(MOST HIGH) saw it all.</p><p class="nations-note">The ships are named.</p><p class="nations-note">The nations are named.</p><p class="nations-note">The people are named.</p></div>
      </div>
      </div>
      <!-- ENTRIES_ZONE_END -->
      </div>
      </section>
      <!-- FINAL SUMMARY -->
      <section class="doc-section" id="secSummary">
      <div class="section-divider">
      <span class="section-num">FINAL SUMMARY</span>
      <div class="section-title">Complete Fulfillment &#8212; Modern Era: 2006&#8211;2026</div>
      </div>
      <!-- SUMMARY_ZONE_START -->
      <p>All curses align with:</p>
      <ul class="coverage-list">
      <li>1619-Today timeline</li>
      <li>Continuous condition</li>
      <li>YASHARA&#39;AL(Israel) identification</li>
      <li>Historical and modern events</li>
      </ul>
      <!-- SUMMARY_ZONE_END -->
      </section>
      </main>
      </div>
      <button class="scroll-top" id="scrollTopBtn" onclick="window.scrollTo({top:0,behavior:'smooth'})" aria-label="Back to top">&uarr;</button>
      <script>
      function scrollToAnchor(id) {
      const el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (el) el.scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth', block:'start'});
      }
      function toggleEntry(header) {
      header.classList.toggle('open');
      const body = header.nextElementSibling;
      body.classList.toggle('open');
      if (body.classList.contains('open')) {
      const entryId = header.closest('.entry-card').id;
      const navEl = document.getElementById('nav-' + entryId);
      if (navEl) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.entry-nav').forEach(n => n.classList.remove('active'));
      navEl.classList.add('active');
      }
      }
      }
      function toggleScripture(btn) {
      btn.classList.toggle('open');
      btn.nextElementSibling.classList.toggle('open');
      }
      const sections = ['secIntro','secDisclaimer','secSpeech','secCurses','secSummary'];
      const sectionNames = ['Introduction','Disclaimer','Forms of Speech','DABARAYAM 28','Summary'];
      const progressFill = document.getElementById('progressFill');
      const sectionIndicator = document.getElementById('sectionIndicator');
      const scrollTopBtn = document.getElementById('scrollTopBtn');
      function onScroll() {
      const scrollTop = window.scrollY;
      const docH = document.documentElement.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight;
      progressFill.style.width = Math.min(100,(scrollTop/docH*100)).toFixed(1)+'%';
      scrollTopBtn.classList.toggle('visible', scrollTop > 400);
      let active = 0;
      sections.forEach((id,i) => {
      const el = document.getElementById(id);
      if (el && el.getBoundingClientRect().top < 120) active = i;
      });
      sectionIndicator.textContent = sectionNames[active];
      document.querySelectorAll('[id^="nav-sec"]').forEach(n => n.classList.remove('active'));
      const an = document.getElementById('nav-'+sections[active]);
      if(an) an.classList.add('active');
      }
      window.addEventListener('scroll', onScroll, {passive:true});
      const observer = new IntersectionObserver(entries => {
      entries.forEach(e => { if(e.isIntersecting) e.target.classList.add('visible'); });
      }, {threshold:0.03});
      document.querySelectorAll('.doc-section').forEach(s => observer.observe(s));
      </script>
      </body>
      </html>
